summary: Comprehensive testing of Composition Cleanup feature. All 16 backend tests passed. Frontend tests verified: Admin Fabrics modal uses SELECT dropdown with 20 canonical materials, Vendor Inventory modal uses SELECT dropdown with 20 canonical materials, B2C /fabrics filter shows only canonical compositions (11 materials from DB), fabric detail pages show canonical composition names.
passed_tests: GET /api/composition/options returns exactly 20 canonical materials in correct order
GET /api/fabrics/filter-options compositions array contains ONLY canonical names (no typos)
POST /api/migrate/compositions requires admin authentication (401/403 without token)
POST /api/migrate/compositions (dry-run) returns changes_count=0 (migration already applied, idempotent)
POST /api/migrate/compositions?apply=true is idempotent (0 changes when already migrated)
POST /api/fabrics (admin create) with typo composition gets auto-normalized (Cottton→Cotton, Poly→Polyester, Spandex→Lycra)
PUT /api/fabrics/{id} (admin update) with typo composition gets normalized (POlyester→Polyester, Elastane→Lycra, viscose→Viscose)
POST /api/vendor/fabrics (vendor create) with typo composition gets normalized (Lyvra→Lycra, Tencel→Lyocell)
PUT /api/vendor/fabrics/{id} (vendor update) normalizes composition (RPET→Recycled Polyester, Flex→Lycra, org cotton→Organic Cotton)
normalize_material() handles all typos correctly (14 test cases)
normalize_material() falls back to title case for unknown strings
canonicalize_composition() preserves integer percentages as int
canonicalize_composition() parses legacy string format ('Cotton 78%, Poly 20%, Spandex 2%')
canonicalize_composition() sorts output by percentage descending
frontend_tests_passed: Admin /admin/fabrics → Add Fabric modal: composition-material-0 is SELECT element (not INPUT)
Admin composition dropdown has exactly 20 canonical options
Admin composition dropdown contains Cotton, Polyester, Lycra, Lyocell in correct order
Admin composition dropdown has NO typos (Cottton, Poly, Lyvra, Lyocel)
Admin selecting Lycra activates stretch mode (amber highlight on dropdown)
Vendor /vendor/inventory → Add Fabric modal: composition editor uses SELECT dropdowns
Vendor composition dropdown has exactly 20 canonical options
Vendor composition dropdown contains Cotton, Polyester, Lycra, Lyocell
Vendor composition dropdown has NO typos
B2C /fabrics → Filters panel: COMPOSITION dropdown shows 11 canonical materials from DB
B2C composition filter options: Cotton, Hemp, Linen, Lurex, Lycra, Lyocell, Organic Cotton, Polyester, Recycled Cotton, Recycled Polyester, Viscose
B2C composition filter has NO typos (Cottton, Poly, Coitton, Lyvra, POlyester, viscose, Lyocel, ORG COTTON)
Fabric detail page shows canonical composition (e.g., '100% Cotton')
critical_code_review_comments: composition_utils.py is well-structured as single source of truth for canonical materials
ALIAS_MAP covers ~60 known typos/synonyms with lowercase keys for case-insensitive matching
normalize_material() gracefully falls back to title case for unknown materials
canonicalize_composition() properly merges duplicates and sorts by percentage descending
fabric_router.py correctly normalizes composition on both create and update
vendor_router.py uses _canon_comp_or_raw() helper for consistent normalization
filter-options endpoint whitelists against CANONICAL_COMPOSITIONS to prevent typo leakage
useCompositionOptions.js hook has module-level cache and hardcoded fallback for resilience
seed_data_creation: None - used existing migrated fabric data
context_for_next_testing_agent: Composition cleanup feature fully tested. Migration was already applied (97 of 194 fabrics updated). All composition inputs now use SELECT dropdowns with 20 canonical materials. Typos like Cottton, Poly, Lyvra, Lyocel are auto-normalized on write. Filter-options API returns only canonical compositions present in DB. The CANONICAL_COMPOSITIONS list in composition_utils.py is the single source of truth.
